path_cfg_ssa_symbolic.note: Modules exist and are tested, but are not yet invoked in the standard runtime detector pipeline.

findings.title: Tautological comparison in staking.spend: datum.mint_policy_id == datum.mint_policy_id
findings.description: Handler staking.spend compares `datum.mint_policy_id == datum.mint_policy_id` — this is always True and likely a copy-paste bug. The intended validation is silently bypassed.
findings.suggestion: Replace one side of the comparison with the intended variable or parameter.

evidence.details: SMT inconclusive: Expression contains constructs beyond the simple solver's capability (quantifiers, arrays, bit-vectors, or nonlinear arithmetic).

findings.title: Multiple quantity_of calls in staking.spend may allow double-counting
findings.description: Handler staking.spend calls `quantity_of` 5 times to check asset quantities. Without verifying mutual exclusivity, an attacker may satisfy multiple checks with overlapping assets.
findings.suggestion: Use `value.tokens` or `value.without_lovelace` for exact value matching instead of multiple independent quantity_of checks.

evidence.details: SMT inconclusive: Expression contains constructs beyond the simple solver's capability (quantifiers, arrays, bit-vectors, or nonlinear arithmetic).

findings.title: Multiple quantity_of calls in staking.mint may allow double-counting
findings.description: Handler staking.mint calls `quantity_of` 7 times to check asset quantities. Without verifying mutual exclusivity, an attacker may satisfy multiple checks with overlapping assets.
findings.suggestion: Use `value.tokens` or `value.without_lovelace` for exact value matching instead of multiple independent quantity_of checks.

evidence.details: SMT inconclusive: Expression contains constructs beyond the simple solver's capability (quantifiers, arrays, bit-vectors, or nonlinear arithmetic).

findings.title: Time-sensitive datum but no validity_range check in staking.spend
findings.description: Datum field(s) [staked_at] suggest time-sensitive logic but handler never accesses transaction.validity_range.
findings.suggestion: Check validity_range against the time field(s) to enforce temporal constraints.

evidence.details: Exploit scenario generated for runtime simulation; Exploit transaction rejected by validator: UPLC evaluation failed: EmptyList(Con(ProtoList(Data, []))). This may indicate a false positive for missing-validity-range.

findings.title: Incomplete Value check via quantity_of in staking.mint
findings.description: Handler staking.mint uses quantity_of to check specific asset quantities but doesn't validate the full Value. Other native assets may be drained.
findings.suggestion: Complement quantity_of checks with assets.match for full Value validation.

findings.title: Withdrawal existence-only check in staking.spend
findings.description: Handler staking.spend checks withdrawal existence but not the amount. An attacker can invoke the staking script with a 0-amount withdrawal.
findings.suggestion: Verify the withdrawal amount is > 0 or matches an expected value.
